caption:
4 import map patterns that kill your bundler.

Ship ES modules directly to the browser. No webpack. No Vite. No build step. Just a script tag.

All browsers since 2023.

📌 SAVE this — your next side project doesn't need a bundler.

Would you ship without a bundler? 👇

#javascript #importmaps #webdev #coding #programming #developer #esmodules #nobundler #html #codingtips

slides.title: Import map patterns that kill your bundler
slides.subtitle: No webpack. No Vite. No build step. Just HTML.

slides.title: Bare specifiers — import 'react' in the browser
slides.desc: Map package names to URLs. No bundler resolves them — the browser does.

slides.title: CDN imports — zero npm install
slides.desc: Point to esm.sh, unpkg, or jsDelivr. No node_modules folder.

slides.title: Scoped overrides — different versions per path
slides.desc: One module uses React 18, another uses React 19. No conflicts.

slides.title: Local files — your own modules
slides.desc: Mix CDN packages with your local code. Same import syntax.
slides.punchline: CDN + local. Same syntax. No config.

slides.title: Import Maps Cheat Sheet
slides.question: Would you ship without a bundler? 👇
